journal_entry.rationale: Inventory is an asset account that increases when goods are purchased for resale. Under IFRS, inventory is measured at the lower of cost and net realizable value. The $100 cash payment represents the cost of acquisition. Cash (an asset) decreases by the same amount. Debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) requires 1 asset increase line = Inventory debit. Credit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) requires 1 asset decrease line = Cash credit. No tax information provided, so assuming the amount is as stated (either tax-exempt inventory or amount already includes any applicable taxes).

output.reason: Purchasing inventory increases the Inventory asset account. This is 1 asset increase on the debit side.

debit_classifier.reason: Purchasing inventory increases the Inventory asset account. This is 1 asset increase on the debit side.

output.reason: Cash is leaving the business to pay for inventory. Cash is an asset, so when it decreases we credit it. This results in one asset decrease credit line.

credit_classifier.reason: Cash is leaving the business to pay for inventory. Cash is an asset, so when it decreases we credit it. This results in one asset decrease credit line.
